title: Explore first-run coach mark for connection gesture
description:
Exploratory bead, not committed. Paul has not decided whether to ship a first-run coach mark teaching the new connection gesture from contextflow-twa (GH #22, draw.io-style hover stubs + whole-shape drop). Filing so the idea is not lost while the implemented stubs are evaluated in real workshop use.

Concept: a one-shot floating callout anchored to a stub on the first context a user hovers, with a short message teaching the drag gesture, dismissable via "Got it" button or auto-dismissed on first successful relationship creation. Persisted via localStorage. Reset surface for workshop facilitators.

Mockup: docs/mockups/connection-ux-mockup.html, Panel 4, on branch mockup/connection-ux. Demonstrates the proposed placement, dismissal, and replay/reset.

Proposed details (all up for revision):
- Trigger: project has >= 2 contexts AND 0 relationships AND user hovers any context for the first time.
- Anchor: floating callout beside the source card's right stub, with a pointer triangle. Re-anchors if the card moves.
- Wording: "Drag any arrow from this context to another to map the relationship. After releasing, pick a DDD pattern." Direction-neutral, consistent with contextflow-twa's tooltip wording.
- Dismissal: "Got it" button, OR auto-dismiss on first relationship creation.
- Persistence: localStorage.contextflow_coach_connection_seen = true.
- Reset: a control under Help / Workshop Mode (location TBD) that clears the flag for fresh demos.
- Visual register: matches GettingStartedGuideModal (slate body, blue accent, no emojis).

Open questions before committing:
- Does it add real value, or does the affordance (hover-revealed arrow stubs + per-stub tooltip + updated rich tooltip) already teach itself? Evaluate after contextflow-twa ships and gets some workshop or self-use time.
- Is anchoring to one stub the right placement, or should the callout sit elsewhere (above the card, in a canvas corner with an arrow drawn to the affordance, etc.)?
- Should the trigger include "user has at least seen one example project" so brand-new users meet the welcome guide first, then the coach mark on their own project?
- Coach mark family: this is the first one; if accepted, does ContextFlow grow a small coach-mark system (CoachMark.tsx component, useCoachMark hook) for future first-run teaches?

Acceptance criteria are not finalized -- decide first whether to ship at all. If yes, work this bead by writing acceptance criteria from the chosen behavior, then implement.

Depends on contextflow-twa (the affordance must exist before there is anything to teach).

title: Remove unused exports from mockState test fixture
description: 5 of 8 exports in src/model/actions/__testFixtures__/mockState.ts are now unused after dead action file removal: createMockGroup, createMockRelationship, createMockKeyframe, setupAnalyticsMock, setupConsoleSpy. Only createBaseMockProject, createMockContext, and createMockState are still imported.

title: Track project export events
description:
Project import tracks project_imported but export has no analytics.

The handleExport function in TopBar.tsx (line 86) builds a JSON blob and triggers a download. No trackEvent call.

Fix: Add trackEvent('project_exported', project) in handleExport, before the download trigger. The project object is already available in scope. No additional metadata needed beyond the standard project metadata (context_count, relationship_count, etc.) that trackEvent includes automatically.

title: ARCHITECTURE.md: update EditorState and component descriptions
description:
The EditorState interface and component descriptions in ARCHITECTURE.md are significantly incomplete:

EditorState interface (compare ARCHITECTURE.md lines 326-349 with src/model/storeTypes.ts lines 45-93):
- Missing selection states: selectedUserId, selectedUserNeedId, selectedUserNeedConnectionId, selectedNeedContextConnectionId, selectedStageIndex, selectedTeamId, selectedContextIds (multi-select array), hoveredContextId, isDragging
- Missing view options: showGroups, showRelationships, showIssueLabels, showTeamLabels, showRelationshipLabels, showHelpTooltips, groupOpacity, colorByMode ('strategic' | 'ownership')
- Missing temporal state: temporal object with currentDate, activeKeyframeId
- canvasView missing distillation viewport (see contextflow-w1y)
- ViewMode type missing 'distillation' (see contextflow-w1y)

Component layout section (lines 209-222):
- ProjectPicker no longer exists; replace with ProjectListPage (src/components/ProjectListPage.tsx)
- InspectorPanel selection routing only mentions context/relationship/group - missing user, userNeed, userNeedConnection, needContextConnection, stage, team selections
- Missing TeamSidebar in component tree (left sidebar is now tabbed: Repos tab and Teams tab with search/filter)
- Component tree missing overlay components (IssueLabelsOverlay, TeamLabelsOverlay, DistillationRegions)

title: Arrow tip occluded by target context border
description: The relationship arrow tip lands exactly at the geometric box edge after #24, but the box border is drawn at that edge with non-zero stroke width, so the last 1-2px of the arrow point is hidden under the border. Most visible on external contexts (dashed ring) and on hover/selected states with thicker strokes. Fix options: (1) push the arrow tip out beyond the box edge by the target borders effective half-stroke-width, computed per node from rendered styles; (2) extend the arrow length and increase the path shortening by the same amount so the tip protrudes past the border with the marker base still meeting the path tail. Independent of GH #21 and #24; surfaces only after #24 made the tip land precisely at the edge.

title: Enrich store analytics metadata for delete/add events
description: The store's trackEvent calls for context_added, context_deleted, user_deleted, and user_need_deleted are missing useful metadata that the old (now-deleted) action files had. Add: context_type and ownership to context_added; relationship_count and group_count to context_deleted; connection_count to user_deleted; user_connection_count and context_connection_count to user_need_deleted. The project data is already in scope at each call site.

title: Track undo/redo usage
description:
The undo and redo actions in store.ts (lines 1030-1037) have no analytics. They delegate to Yjs UndoManager via getCollabUndoRedo().

Fix: Add undo_used and redo_used events. The Yjs UndoManager does not expose what action type was undone, so metadata is limited to a simple event fire. Do not attempt to track action_type or stack_depth; the underlying Yjs undo stack is opaque from the store layer.

This reveals how often users rely on undo/redo as a safety net.

title: Teams UI not surfacing in bounded context inspector
description:
CONFIRMED BUG. In a fresh project, there is no way to assign teams to bounded contexts.

The ContextInspector shows: Ownership (Our Team/Internal/External), Legacy, Big Ball of Mud, Role, Code size, Boundary, Notes, Issues, Relationships. No team assignment dropdown appears.

Additionally, the left sidebar with Repos/Teams tabs only appears when repos or teams already exist (e.g. in the ACME example project). In a new project, there is no sidebar and no way to create teams.

**Root cause investigation needed:**
- The team assignment dropdown code exists in ContextInspector.tsx (lines 207-234) but is not rendering
- Check if it's conditionally hidden when no teams exist in the project
- Check if the sidebar "Teams" tab with the "Add Team" button is hidden when the team count is 0
- The left sidebar (RepoSidebar/TeamSidebar) needs to be accessible even in empty projects so users can create their first team

**Key files:**
- `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx` lines 207-234 (team dropdown)
- `src/components/TeamSidebar.tsx` (team creation + sidebar tab)
- `src/components/RepoSidebar.tsx` (repo sidebar tab)
- `src/components/CanvasArea.tsx` (sidebar rendering logic)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Team assignment dropdown visible in ContextInspector when teams exist
- Sidebar accessible in empty projects so users can create teams
- Creating a team and assigning it to a context works end-to-end

GH issue: https://github.com/virtualgenius/contextflow/issues/4

title: Repository assignment UI not visible in bounded context inspector
description:
CONFIRMED BUG. In a fresh project, there is no way to assign repos to bounded contexts.

The ContextInspector does not show any repo assignment section. The left sidebar with Repos/Teams tabs only appears when repos or teams already exist. In a new project, there is no sidebar and no way to create or assign repos.

Repo assignment works via drag-and-drop from the RepoSidebar in projects that already have repos (e.g. ACME example), but there's no way to get to the RepoSidebar in a fresh project.

**Root cause:** Same as contextflow-4is. The sidebar is conditionally hidden when no repos/teams exist, creating a chicken-and-egg problem: you can't create repos because the sidebar that lets you create repos isn't visible.

**Key files:**
- `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx` (repo display section)
- `src/components/RepoSidebar.tsx` (repo creation + drag-and-drop)
- `src/components/CanvasArea.tsx` (sidebar rendering logic)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Sidebar accessible in empty projects so users can create repos
- Repo assignment via drag-and-drop works end-to-end from fresh project
- Assigned repos visible in ContextInspector

GH issue: https://github.com/virtualgenius/contextflow/issues/5

title: UX_GUIDELINES.md: fix outdated persistence model and future items
description:
UX Guidelines has several items that contradict the current state of the app:

Persistence and Behavior section (line 148):
- Says 'Local-first persistence: IndexedDB storage with Miro-style autosave' - should describe cloud-first persistence with Yjs/Cloudflare Durable Objects. IndexedDB is now only used for migration backup.

Canvas Behavior section (line 52):
- Says 'Autosave: Changes persist automatically to IndexedDB (Miro-like)' - should say changes sync to cloud automatically via Yjs.

Future UX Enhancements section (lines 162-171):
- 'Collaborative editing: Real-time multi-user editing with conflict resolution' listed as future but already implemented (Yjs CRDT + Cloudflare Workers). Remove from future list.
- 'Keyboard shortcuts overlay: Discoverable documentation for power users' listed as future but already implemented (KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx, Cmd/Ctrl+?). Remove from future list.

Component Roles table (lines 125-134):
- Missing ProjectListPage.tsx (landing page/project list)
- Missing overlay components (IssueLabelsOverlay, TeamLabelsOverlay, DistillationRegions, etc.)
- Missing TeamSidebar mention (left sidebar is now tabbed: Repos/Teams)

title: README.md: fix outdated project status, examples, and planned features
description:
Several items in README.md are out of date:

Planned section:
- 'Keyboard shortcuts documentation overlay' listed under Planned but already implemented (Cmd/Ctrl+?, see KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx). Remove from Planned.

Getting Started example projects:
- 'Empty Project' listed as example project but was removed (commit b0dfdf9). Remove from list.

Project Structure section:
- References examples/empty.project.json which no longer exists. Remove from listing.
- References 'elan.project.json' but actual filename is 'elan-warranty.project.json'. Fix the filename.

Project Status section:
- Shows 'M6: Organic blob-based group rendering (in progress)' but M6 is complete (blobShape.ts fully implemented with Catmull-Rom smoothing). Change to checkmark.
- Consider updating the status blurb since M1-M6 are all done.

Contributing section:
- Discord is only shown as a header badge. Add a mention of the Discord community in the Contributing section or elsewhere in the body text so it's discoverable when reading (not just via the badge).

title: Default anonymous usage analytics to on
description: Anonymous usage analytics (PostHog) should be on by default for new users, with a clear opt-out in settings. Current state: verify whether analytics is opt-in or opt-out and what the default is. Goal: collect usage signal by default without surprising users; respect any existing PostHog opt-out preference; surface the toggle and a short explanation of what is and is not collected (no PII, no project content). Tasks: (1) audit current analytics init path and default flag, (2) ensure default is enabled for new users while preserving existing opt-outs, (3) add or refine the settings UI toggle with copy explaining anonymous-only scope, (4) document in README/UX docs.

title: Add keyboard navigation and ARIA roles to PillGroup
description:
Make the PillGroup component (introduced in contextflow-l4i) keyboard accessible.

Requirements:
- Role="radiogroup" on the container, role="radio" on each pill, aria-checked reflects active state
- Field label associated via aria-labelledby
- Arrow keys (Left/Right/Up/Down) move focus between pills and update selection (radiogroup pattern)
- Home/End jump to first/last pill
- Visible focus ring distinct from active state

Scope: PillGroup in src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx and any callers (ContextInspector pill fields: Ownership, Role, Code Size, Boundary).

Out of scope: keyboard nav for other inspector controls; reworking the Team dropdown.

Depends on contextflow-l4i (PillGroup must exist first).

title: Add close button to all details/overlay panels missing one
description: Several details and overlay panels lack an explicit close affordance (X button), forcing users to click outside or guess at dismissal. Audit and add a consistent close control to every floating/overlay panel that does not already have one. Known candidates to verify: View Options panel, Context Details (Inspector), Relationship panel, any other side panels or popovers. Acceptance: every dismissable panel has a visible close button in a consistent position (top-right), keyboard-dismissable (Esc), with the same icon and hover treatment. Out of scope: redesigning panel layout (see contextflow-l4i for Inspector redesign).

title: Per-pill tooltips explaining each enum value
description:
Add hover tooltips to individual pills in PillGroup so users see what each enum value means without consulting external docs. Especially useful for Role (Revenue Generator vs Engagement Creator) and Boundary Integrity (what makes a boundary weak vs strong).

Requirements:
- Each pill option in PillGroup accepts an optional description string
- Wrap pill in InfoTooltip (if description is a ConceptDefinition) or SimpleTooltip (if plain text); respect existing tooltip conventions documented in CLAUDE.md
- For the four pill fields in ContextInspector, supply concise per-option descriptions:
  - Ownership: Our Team / Internal (other team in our org) / External (third party)
  - Role: short description of each business model role
  - Code Size: rough LOC ranges for each bucket (tiny <1k, small 1-10k, medium 10-50k, large 50-200k, huge 200k+)
  - Boundary: what makes the boundary weak/moderate/strong
- Tooltips on Role and Boundary use InfoTooltip (educational DDD concepts); Ownership and Code Size use SimpleTooltip (descriptive only)

Scope: PillGroup component and the four pill field call sites in ContextInspector.

Out of scope: tooltips on other inspector controls; rewriting existing tooltip components.

Depends on contextflow-l4i (PillGroup must exist first).

title: Track connection deletion events
description:
Connection creation events are tracked (user_need_connection_created, need_context_connection_created) but deletions are not.

Store actions with no analytics (store.ts):
- deleteUserNeedConnection (line 808): just calls getCollabMutations().deleteUserNeedConnection()
- deleteNeedContextConnection (line 845): just calls getCollabMutations().deleteNeedContextConnection()

Fix: Add user_need_connection_deleted and need_context_connection_deleted events in the store actions. Include entity_type and entity_id metadata to match the creation pattern.

Note: userActions.ts has deleteUserNeedConnectionAction and deleteNeedContextConnectionAction functions, but the store does not use them; it calls collab mutations directly. The tracking should go in the store actions.

title: Audit and upgrade iconography across the app
description:
Replace remaining inline SVG icons with a consistent iconset. Project already depends on lucide-react (used in App.tsx, TopBar.tsx, ImportConflictDialog.tsx, TeamSidebar.tsx, KeyframeManager.tsx and elsewhere), but ~28 inline SVGs remain scattered across components (counted via grep "<svg" src/ -r).

Step 1: audit
- Inventory all remaining inline SVGs (grep "<svg" src/ -r) and categorize: navigation/action icons (plus, x, chevron, etc.), domain-specific icons (arrows for relationships, user/team glyphs), and decorative.
- For each inline SVG, identify the lucide-react equivalent. Note any gaps where lucide does not have a good match.

Step 2: decide
- Recommended default: consolidate on lucide-react (already a dependency, broad coverage, consistent stroke style). Replace inline SVGs with lucide equivalents.
- If audit reveals significant gaps for domain-specific icons (e.g., DDD relationship glyphs, ContextFlow-specific marks), evaluate either keeping those as custom inline SVGs in a dedicated src/components/icons/ module, or adopting a complementary iconset (phosphor-icons or @radix-ui/react-icons).

Step 3: replace
- Migrate inline SVGs to the chosen iconset
- Centralize any retained custom icons in src/components/icons/
- Ensure stroke width and size are consistent (lucide default stroke-width=2)
- Update mockups (docs/mockups/*.html, docs/inspector-panel-mockup.html) to use the same icons (or note the divergence)

Scope: visual consistency only. No behavioral changes.

Out of scope: rebranding, changing the visual identity, restyling icon containers.

Note: this may also touch the inspector redesign output from contextflow-l4i. Consider sequencing so l4i lands first to avoid merge friction.

title: ARCHITECTURE.md: update data model to match types.ts
description:
The data model code block in ARCHITECTURE.md is significantly out of date compared to src/model/types.ts. Key discrepancies:

Project interface:
- Shows obsolete Actor/ActorConnection; actual code uses User/UserNeed/UserNeedConnection/NeedContextConnection (4 interfaces replacing 2)
- Missing metadata fields: version, isBuiltIn, createdAt, updatedAt

BoundedContext interface:
- Still shows 'isExternal?: boolean' which NO LONGER EXISTS in types.ts. It was replaced by 'ownership?: ContextOwnership' where ContextOwnership = 'ours' | 'internal' | 'external'
- Missing new fields: businessModelRole ('revenue-generator' | 'engagement-creator' | 'compliance-enforcer' | 'cost-reduction'), isBigBallOfMud (boolean), teamId (string, direct team assignment orthogonal to repo ownership)

FlowStageMarker:
- Field name wrong: doc says 'label' but code uses 'name'
- Missing fields: description?, owner?, notes?

Other missing types:
- Issue interface (id, title, description?, severity) and issues field on BoundedContext
- ContextOwnership type
- User, UserNeed, UserNeedConnection, NeedContextConnection interfaces (replacements for Actor/ActorConnection)

title: ACL and OHS boxes on the same context side overlap
description:
When a single bounded context plays both roles for relationships that resolve to the same side, the two indicator boxes render in the same position and overlap.

Concrete setup that triggers it:
- Context X is the downstream end of an ACL relationship with context A.
- Context X is the upstream end of an OHS relationship with context B.
- A and B are both positioned such that the indicator auto-relocation logic picks the same side of X for each (e.g., both relationships use X's right side).
- The ACL box hugs X on that side, and the OHS box hugs X on the same side. They overlap.

This is rare in workshop diagrams (most contexts only play one role per side), but it is reachable, and the current layout has no handling for the collision.

Scope notes:
- Affects only ACL/OHS indicator boxes, not the relationship lines themselves (those are routed independently per edge).
- Related but distinct from contextflow-ki1 (two-sided relationship pattern modeling). This bead is purely about box layout, not relationship semantics.
- Builds on the geometry refinement landed in contextflow-if3.

Possible approaches to discuss before implementing:
1. Stack the two boxes along the perpendicular to the attachment edge (e.g., ACL above OHS on a right-side attachment), with the relationship lines updated to hit each box's outer midpoint.
2. Nudge one of the indicator boxes to the next-best side of the same context (the next side that still faces the other endpoint reasonably).
3. Combine into a single dual-label indicator when the two relationships are between the same pair of contexts (this would also cover the two-sided pattern case at the layout layer, even if contextflow-ki1 has not modeled it yet).

Acceptance criteria (to refine during design):
- When two indicators land on the same side of the same context, both are visible without overlap.
- Both relationship lines still attach to the outer-edge midpoint of their respective box and leave perpendicular to that edge.
- Auto-relocation continues to work as either context is dragged; the collision-handling rule re-applies on each layout pass.

Mockup work likely needed before code (project convention: visual changes get HTML mockups in docs/mockups/ first).

title: Track view preference toggles
description:
Settings toggles give insight into how users customize their view but none are tracked.

Store actions with no analytics (store.ts):
- toggleShowGroups (line 855)
- toggleShowRelationships (line 861)
- toggleIssueLabels (line 867)
- toggleTeamLabels (line 873)
- toggleRelationshipLabels (line 879)
- toggleHelpTooltips (line 885)
- setGroupOpacity (line 902)
- setColorByMode (line 907)

Fix: Add a single view_preference_changed event in each action. Include preference_name (string enum) and new_value (boolean for toggles, string for colorByMode, number for opacity). These are all safe to track (no PII). Helps answer: which visual layers do users actually use?

title: Represent Shared Kernel as overlapping contexts (GH #23)
description: Visual notation change: Shared Kernel renders as two overlapping context shapes with the kernel in the intersection region (hatching/tint), no connecting line. Aligns with Eric Evans canonical context map notation. Resolves the open overlap question from #21 (overlap is reserved for Shared Kernel; the edge algorithm only handles non-overlapping cases). Open design question on creation UX: drag-to-overlap creates the relationship, vs. relationship-first with positioning hint. See GH #23.

title: Repos sidebar tab should show all repos, not just unassigned
description:
The Repos sidebar tab only appears when unassigned repos exist, but the Teams tab shows all teams regardless. Make Repos consistent: show all repos (assigned and unassigned) so the tab appears whenever any repos exist in the project.

## Current behavior
- `App.tsx:94-96`: `unassignedRepos` filters to `!r.contextId`
- `App.tsx:109`: `hasUnassignedRepos` gates the Repos tab visibility
- `App.tsx:284`: `RepoSidebar` receives only unassigned repos
- When all repos are assigned, the Repos tab disappears entirely
- Tab header says "Unassigned Repos (N)" in single-tab mode

## Required changes

### 1. App.tsx
- Add a `hasRepos` check: `(project?.repos?.length ?? 0) > 0`
- Replace `hasUnassignedRepos` with `hasRepos` in `hasLeftSidebarContent` and sidebar visibility logic
- Pass ALL repos to RepoSidebar: `repos={project?.repos || []}` instead of `repos={unassignedRepos}`
- Update auto-tab-switching logic (lines 115-120) to use `hasRepos` instead of `hasUnassignedRepos`
- Update tab labels: "Repos (N)" everywhere (drop "Unassigned" prefix)
- Keep `unassignedRepos` memo if needed for count display, or remove if no longer used

### 2. RepoSidebar.tsx
- Show assigned repos alongside unassigned ones
- Visually distinguish assigned repos: show which BoundedContext they belong to (e.g., a subtle label or chip with the context name)
- Assigned repos should NOT be draggable (they're already placed)
- Unassigned repos remain draggable for drag-and-drop assignment
- Consider grouping: unassigned repos first (ready to assign), then assigned repos below

### 3. Store / data access
- RepoSidebar will need access to context names to display assignments. Either:
  - Pass a `contexts` prop (or a `contextMap: Record<string, string>` of id->name), OR
  - Have RepoSidebar read from the store directly via `useEditorStore`
- Prefer the store approach to match TeamSidebar's pattern

## Edge cases
- Repo with contextId pointing to a deleted context: treat as unassigned
- External contexts: repos can't be assigned to them, but if somehow assigned, still show the assignment

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Repos tab appears whenever project has any repos (assigned or not)
- [ ] Unassigned repos are draggable; assigned repos are not
- [ ] Assigned repos show their context name
- [ ] Tab count reflects total repo count, not just unassigned
- [ ] Left sidebar auto-tab-switching works correctly with new logic
close_reason: Implemented: repos tab shows all repos, assigned repos display context name and are non-draggable, section headers when both types present

title: Add analytics tracking for untracked user actions
description:
Add trackEvent calls for high-value user actions that are currently untracked:

**High Value:**
1. Issue management (issue_added, issue_deleted, issue_updated with severity)
2. Import conflict resolution (track replace vs import-as-new choice)
3. Temporal navigation (keyframe_selected, timeline_date_changed)
4. Getting Started Guide opened (FTUE signal)
5. Keyboard Shortcuts modal opened (help-seeking behavior)

**Medium Value:**
6. Share dialog opened (compare with share_link_copied for drop-off)
7. CodeCohesion integration toggled (feature adoption)
8. Sidebar tab switches (repos vs teams panel usage)

Respect PII - no text content, no URLs, just event names and counts/types. Use TDD where the tracking logic involves conditional behavior (e.g., the issue management events that carry severity metadata).
close_reason: Added analytics tracking for 8 untracked user actions

title: Add Business Model Role field to bounded contexts
description: Add a businessModelRole dropdown to the BoundedContext entity and Inspector panel. Four options: revenue-generator, engagement-creator, compliance-enforcer, cost-reduction. This captures HOW a context contributes to the business, complementing the existing strategic classification (WHAT kind of domain). Include an InfoTooltip with educational content explaining each role, following the existing tooltip pattern (ConceptDefinition, respects showHelpTooltips). From DDD Crew Bounded Context Canvas V5.

title: Track share link generation
description:
The ShareProjectDialog component (src/components/ShareProjectDialog.tsx) lets users generate and copy collaboration links but has no analytics.

Key functions:
- getShareUrl (line 12): generates the share URL
- handleCopy (line 53): copies URL to clipboard

Fix: Add a single share_link_copied event in handleCopy. The share URL is generated on dialog open (not a separate user action), so tracking the copy is the meaningful signal. Include project metadata (pass project to trackEvent). Do NOT include the actual URL in the event (PII policy: no full URLs).

title: Track entity property update events
description:
Entity creation and deletion are well-tracked, but property updates are inconsistently covered. Context property changes ARE tracked via context_property_changed and trackTextFieldEdit in contextActions.ts. The same pattern should extend to other entity types.

Store actions with no update tracking:
- updateGroup (store.ts:512): group name, color, notes
- updateTeam (store.ts:615): team name
- updateUser (store.ts:696): user name, type (internal/external)
- updateUserNeed (store.ts:759): need text, visibility
- updateKeyframe (store.ts:1250): keyframe label, date
- updateFlowStage (store.ts:914): stage name, color
- updateUserNeedConnection (store.ts:813): connection properties
- updateNeedContextConnection (store.ts:850): connection properties

Implementation approach: Follow the contextActions.ts pattern:
- Use trackPropertyChange for enum/boolean changes (e.g., user type, need visibility)
- Use trackTextFieldEdit for text fields (PII-safe: char counts only, never actual text)
- Each update action receives an updates object; check which keys changed and track accordingly

PII rules apply: never track actual names, text content, or descriptions. Only track field_name, old/new char counts, and enum values.

title: ACL/OHS indicator box: refine edge geometry, perpendicular tangent, and arrow placement
description:
Refine the geometry of relationship lines where they meet ACL/OHS indicator boxes. Interactive mockup: docs/mockups/acl-ohs-box-geometry-mockup.html.

CODEBASE CONTEXT (already in place, do NOT change unless noted):
- Indicator box rendering and ACL/OHS rendered path live in src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx. The path computation that feeds the rendered ACL/OHS paths sits in the ~100-160 range; the rendered aclOhsPath blocks (ACL branch, OHS branch) sit in the ~180-210 range; the indicator box <rect>+<text> rendering sits in the ~228+ range. Treat line numbers as orientation hints only.
- Indicator placement is driven by PATTERN_EDGE_INDICATORS in src/lib/canvasConstants.ts. Important convention used by this code: ACL uses position: "source", and the source end of the edge is the downstream context. OHS uses position: "target", and the target end of the edge is the upstream context. (Project rule: arrow always points to upstream, i.e., to the target end of the edge.)
- Existing helpers: getIndicatorBoxPosition (src/lib/edgeUtils.ts); getBoxEdgePoint, shortenEdgeEndpoint (src/lib/edgeGeometry.ts); getEdgeParams.
- Existing constants: EDGE_ENDPOINT_GAP = 6 (the small gap used at context-box attachments); ARROW_MARKER_LENGTH (marker size + gap).
- Indicator auto-relocation around the parent context as contexts are moved is ALREADY implemented (via getEdgeParams + getIndicatorBoxPosition). The mockup confirms this is the desired behavior; preserve it.

WHAT TO CHANGE (geometry rules, all visible in the mockup):
1. The indicator box hugs its parent context with a ~2px visual gap. No relationship line segment is drawn between the indicator box and its parent context.
2. The relationship line attaches to the outer edge of the indicator box: the side facing AWAY from the parent context. This is a locked rule, not "nearest edge to the other endpoint." (Mockup mode: "Proposed B: opposite from context.")
3. The attachment point is the midpoint of the chosen outer edge (vertical center for left/right edges; horizontal center for top/bottom).
4. The visible path is shortened so the tip sits in a small gap before the box border, using the same EDGE_ENDPOINT_GAP value already used at context-box attachments.
5. The curve style is a tangent-at-edge bezier at BOTH endpoints (the indicator-box end AND the non-indicator context end). At each endpoint, the bezier control point lies along the outward normal of the attachment edge at that endpoint, so the line leaves and enters perpendicular to that edge. Each endpoint gets its own control point, placed at a distance from that endpoint approximately equal to max(60, D * 0.5), where D is the Euclidean distance between the two attachment points. Strong enough that a clear perpendicular stub is visible at each end before the curve bends. Tune to match the mockup.
6. Do NOT use orthogonal/elbow paths. The project preserves a hand-drawn bezier aesthetic on relationship edges.
7. Arrow head placement: the arrow is always at the TARGET (upstream) end of the edge. Concretely:
   - ACL pattern (position: "source"): the target is the upstream context (the non-ACL context). The arrow sits at the end of the line that touches that context, pointing into the context. No arrow head appears at, or pointing at, the ACL box.
   - OHS pattern (position: "target"): the target is the upstream context, and the OHS box sits on it. The arrow sits at the OHS-box end of the line, pointing into the box.
8. Rules apply to ACL and OHS, on all four sides of the parent context (left, right, top, bottom).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (each item is observable canvas behavior; a worker should be able to write a failing scenario-level behavioral test from these alone):
- Given an ACL relationship is visible on the canvas, the ACL box appears flush against its parent (downstream) context with no relationship line drawn between them.
- Given an ACL relationship, the relationship line meets the ACL box at the midpoint of the outer edge of the box (the side facing away from the parent context), with a small visible gap before the box border.
- Given an ACL relationship, the line leaves the ACL box perpendicular to its outer edge (a visible horizontal or vertical stub before the curve bends) and enters the other context perpendicular to the context edge it meets.
- Given an ACL relationship, the arrow head appears at the upstream-context end of the line, pointing into that context. No arrow head appears at, or pointing at, the ACL box.
- All of the above hold for OHS relationships symmetrically, with the arrow head at the OHS-box end (pointing into the box).
- When the parent context of an indicator box is dragged so that the other context now lies on a different side of it, the indicator box relocates to that side and every other acceptance criterion above continues to hold (existing relocation behavior preserved).

NOT IN SCOPE:
- Changing the curve style of plain relationship edges that have no ACL or OHS indicator box. This refinement is scoped to ACL/OHS edges only.
- Changing the indicator label, colors, or hover/tooltip behavior. See contextflow-st9 for tooltip layering.
- Two-sided relationship pattern modeling. See contextflow-ki1.
notes: Mockup: docs/mockups/acl-ohs-box-geometry-mockup.html. Three interactive demos (Demo 1 ACL side-attached, Demo 2 OHS side-attached, Demo 3 ACL auto-relocate around the destination context as either context is dragged). Toggle "Attachment edge" defaults to "Opposite from context" and "Curve style" defaults to "Tangent at edge"; these are the chosen target behavior.
close_reason: Implemented ACL/OHS indicator box geometry refinement per docs/mockups/acl-ohs-box-geometry-mockup.html. Box hugs parent at 2px gap, attachment at outer-edge midpoint, tangent-at-edge bezier perpendicular at both endpoints, arrow placement preserved at upstream end.

title: Fix joint side selection for floating edge anchors (GH #21)
description: Bug: getEdgeParams in src/lib/edgeGeometry.ts selects source and target anchor sides independently, producing wrap-around bezier curves when contexts are diagonally positioned (e.g., Top of source paired with Left of target). Replace with joint optimization: enumerate all 16 (sourceSide, targetSide) combinations, compute distance between side midpoints, pick the minimum. See GH issue #21 for details and the Dome Safety -> Central Control example. Display-geometry fix only; no change to creation UX or relationship model. Bezier paths preserved.

title: Support two-sided relationship patterns on context map connections
description: GH #13. Replace single pattern field with standalone patterns (Customer-Supplier, Partnership, Shared Kernel) vs per-side roles (upstream: OHS/PL, downstream: Conformist/ACL). Tom Asel top priority for workshop prep. Design: docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships.md and docs/DDD_RELATIONSHIP_PATTERNS.md. Test plan (double-loop BDD scenarios driving the slice work): docs/PLAN-double-loop-bdd.md.
notes:
When picking this up: reopen the drag-direction-as-direction decision from #22.

Current convention (P1, types.ts:80-86): drag-from=downstream, drag-to=upstream, arrow points to upstream. P1 is in tension with (a) two-sided pattern model (upstreamRole/downstreamRole independent, either may be undefined) and (b) symmetric patterns (Partnership, Shared Kernel, Separate Ways have no direction and render no arrow head).

Likely move: P3 -- drag = topology only; direction set in pattern picker with neither/symmetric/undecided as valid options.

Direction-teaching language is also deferred here from #22. The #22 create-affordance surfaces (stub tooltip, rich card-hover tooltip, first-run coach mark) are direction-neutral. Reintroduce upstream/downstream teaching in this bead, after the model question is resolved -- ideally in the pattern picker and inspector (where the relationship is concrete) rather than the generic create affordance.

See memory: design_drag_direction_convention.md

title: Track group membership changes
description:
Group creation and deletion are tracked (group_created, group_deleted) but membership changes are not.

Store actions with no analytics (store.ts):
- removeContextFromGroup (line 528)
- addContextToGroup (line 533)
- addContextsToGroup (line 538)

All three just call getCollabMutations() with no tracking.

Fix: Add context_added_to_group and context_removed_from_group events. Include member_count (number of contexts in group after the change). Do NOT include group names or context names (PII policy). Use hashProjectId pattern if group_id is needed.

title: Redesign Context Inspector panel layout
description:
GH #15. Layout-only reorganization of ContextInspector. No behavioral changes, no store/type/mutation changes. No changes to enum values; display labels for Role pills are abbreviated for fit (see below).

## Section hierarchy

Reorganize ~20 flat sections into 7 visual groups separated by subtle dividers:
1. Identity (no header): Name, Purpose, Connected Users
2. Strategic Profile: Classification + Evolution badges (single row, with Domain / Evolution mini-labels above), Business Model Role pill group
3. Team & Organization: Ownership pill group, Team dropdown (hidden when ownership=external), Groups chips
4. Codebase: Repos cards, Code Size pill group, Boundary Integrity pill group + Boundary Notes textarea (only shown when boundary is set), Legacy + Big Ball of Mud toggles in a 2-col grid
5. Notes & Issues: Notes textarea, Issues list
6. Relationships: Upstream / Downstream / Mutual + Add Relationship
7. Danger Zone (no header): Delete Context

Temporal Position section continues to render after Strategic Profile when viewMode=strategic and project.temporal.enabled and currentDate is set (current behavior at ContextInspector.tsx:346, preserve as-is).

## Pill groups (replace 4 enum selects)

Enum fields use pill groups, not dropdowns. Team stays a dropdown (user-defined list, not a fixed enum).

| Field | Options (enum value -> display label) | Layout | Active variant |
|---|---|---|---|
| Ownership | ours -> Our Team, internal -> Internal, external -> External | horizontal flex, flex-1 each | green |
| Role | revenue-generator -> Revenue, engagement-creator -> Engagement, compliance-enforcer -> Compliance, cost-reduction -> Cost Reduction | grid-cols-2 (2x2) | green |
| Code Size | tiny / small / medium / large / huge (capitalized) | grid-cols-3 (3+2) | slate |
| Boundary | weak -> Weak, moderate -> Moderate, strong -> Strong | grid-cols-3 | slate |

Enum values are unchanged; only display labels for Role are abbreviated (Revenue Generator -> Revenue, etc.) for pill fit.

## PillGroup component shape

In src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx, add a generic PillGroup that accepts:
- options: array of { value, label, adornment? } where adornment is an optional ReactNode rendered before the label
- value: current selection
- onChange(newValue)
- layout: horizontal | grid-2 | grid-3
- variant: green | slate (controls active background, text, and ring color)

Boundary uses the adornment slot for a 14x10px border swatch (border-style preview); Code Size uses it for a scaled square dot (6px Tiny -> 8 Small -> 10 Medium -> 12 Large -> 14 Huge).

## Pill styling (Tailwind)

Base: px-2 py-1 text-[11px] font-medium rounded
Inactive: bg-slate-100 text-slate-600 dark:bg-neutral-800 dark:text-slate-400
Active (green): bg-green-100 text-green-800 ring-1 ring-green-400 dark:bg-green-900/40 dark:text-green-300 dark:ring-green-600
Active (slate): bg-slate-200 text-slate-900 ring-1 ring-slate-500 dark:bg-neutral-700 dark:text-slate-100 dark:ring-neutral-500
Hover (inactive): bg-slate-200 dark:bg-neutral-700

Field label above the pill row: text-xs text-slate-500 dark:text-slate-400 mb-1, with the existing InfoTooltip help icon inline next to it.

## Other shared components

Add to src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx:
- SectionDivider: <div class="border-t border-slate-200 dark:border-neutral-700 mt-4 pt-4">; container for the next section
- FIELD_LABEL_CLASS constant for consistent inline field labels
- SELECT_CLASS constant retained for the Team picker

## Files

- src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx: add PillGroup, SectionDivider, FIELD_LABEL_CLASS
- src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx: reorganize JSX per the 7-section hierarchy; replace Ownership / Role / Code Size / Boundary controls with PillGroup; move classification + evolution badges to a single row; move Legacy + Big Ball of Mud into a 2-col toggle grid

## Acceptance criteria (observable)

A reviewer opens the app with a context that has many properties populated (e.g., the Payment Processing mockup) and:
1. Sees seven distinct visual groups separated by thin dividers, in the order above.
2. Sees Ownership, Role, Code Size, and Boundary rendered as pill groups (no native select dropdowns for those four fields).
3. Clicking a Boundary pill (Weak / Moderate / Strong) updates the selected context node border on the canvas to dotted-1.5px / solid-2px / solid-3px respectively.
4. Clicking a Code Size pill updates the selected context node width/height on the canvas to the corresponding NODE_SIZES bucket.
5. Clicking a Role pill updates the contexts businessModelRole to the matching enum value (verify by reselecting the context and seeing the same pill highlighted).
6. The Team picker remains a dropdown and continues to hide when ownership = external.
7. Boundary Notes textarea appears only when Boundary has a value.
8. Toggles for Legacy and Big Ball of Mud render side-by-side in one row.
9. Temporal Position section appears after Strategic Profile in Strategic View when temporal mode is enabled with a current date.
10. Dark mode renders all pill states (inactive, hover, active green, active slate) with sufficient contrast.

## Tooling gates

npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all pass with no new failures.

## References

- docs/inspector-panel-redesign.md (full plan)
- docs/inspector-panel-mockup.html (visual reference)

title: Track temporal mode toggle
description:
Temporal keyframe CRUD is tracked (keyframe_created, keyframe_deleted, keyframe_context_position_changed) but the act of toggling temporal mode on/off is not.

Store action: toggleTemporalMode (store.ts:1131)

Fix: Add temporal_mode_toggled event with enabled: true/false (the new state after toggle). This tells us adoption rate of the temporal feature. The project param is available in the action (project is fetched at line 1135).

title: Add first_relationship_added FTUE milestone
description:
The trackFTUEMilestone function defines first_relationship_added but it's never called. The other 3 FTUE milestones are all triggered from store.ts:

- first_context_added (store.ts:410, contextActions.ts:268)
- first_group_created (store.ts:504)
- second_view_discovered (store.ts:237)

Fix: Add trackFTUEMilestone('first_relationship_added', null) after the trackEvent call in addRelationship (store.ts:557). Note: addRelationship currently passes null for the project param; this is consistent with the existing pattern there.

title: Add Big Ball of Mud flag to bounded contexts
description: Add an isBigBallOfMud boolean field to BoundedContext, similar to isLegacy. When enabled, shows a visual badge on the context node (like the Legacy badge). Represents Eric Evans' strategic design pattern: a system with mixed models and inconsistent boundaries that needs isolation. Add to Inspector as a toggle alongside the existing Legacy toggle. Include an InfoTooltip explaining what BBoM means and why it matters (signals need for isolation/decomposition), following the existing tooltip pattern.

title: Temporal/evolution view button is broken
description:
Clicking the temporal button on the strategic view does nothing. Was working earlier this week (visible in a LinkedIn screenshot). Regression.

## Technical Context

The toggle button in TopBar.tsx calls `toggleTemporalMode()` which delegates to `getCollabMutations().toggleTemporal()`. The Yjs mutation in keyframeMutations.ts calls `getTemporalMap(ydoc)` which returns `null` if the temporal property doesn't exist in the Yjs document, causing the toggle to fail silently.

**Likely root causes (investigate in order):**
1. `getTemporalMap()` returning null for projects that lack an initialized temporal object
2. The collab mutation chain breaking somewhere between store → collabStore → Yjs

**Key files:**
- `src/components/TopBar.tsx` - temporal toggle button (only in strategic view)
- `src/components/TimeSlider.tsx` - timeline UI, renders when `project?.temporal?.enabled`
- `src/model/store.ts` - `toggleTemporalMode()` action
- `src/model/sync/keyframeMutations.ts` - `toggleTemporalMutation()`, `getTemporalMap()`

**Note:** KeyframeManager.tsx and KeyframeEditor.tsx exist but are never imported anywhere in the codebase. May be dead code or incomplete wiring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking temporal toggle in strategic view enables the TimeSlider at the bottom of the canvas
- Toggling off hides the TimeSlider
- Works for both new and existing projects

GH issue: https://github.com/virtualgenius/contextflow/issues/3

title: Review ContextFlow PR #20 (ES view from Angel Vazquez)
description: Review and respond to community PR adding EventStorming view to ContextFlow. Analysis saved in products/research/contextflow/2026-03-29-es-view-pr-analysis.md. Need to: (1) do a code review pass, (2) draft a thoughtful reply explaining why it doesn't align with product direction, (3) point Angel toward EventStormer as alternative.

title: Allow much tighter zoom-in on canvas
description: React Flow zoom is currently clamped at maxZoom=2 in src/components/CanvasArea.tsx:1012, which blocks users from zooming in tighter than 200%. Raise the ceiling significantly (e.g., 4-8x) so users can zoom in close to inspect a single context, read small text, or work in dense areas of the map. Also consider whether minZoom (currently 0.1) needs adjustment for very large maps. Verify overlay cutoffs (issue labels, team labels at zoom < 0.4) and node rendering still look reasonable at the new bounds.

title: Bug: dragging contexts in Strategic/Distillation views does not auto-classify
description: Moving a context in Strategic view should auto-update evolutionStage (genesis/custom-built/product/commodity) based on x position. Moving in Distillation view should auto-update strategicClassification (core/supporting/generic). The logic exists in contextActions.ts moveContextAction (lines 113-126) using classifyFromStrategicPosition and classifyFromDistillationPosition, but this is dead code. The store's updateContextPosition just calls getCollabMutations().updateContextPosition() with no classification. The distillation view has partial logic in setViewMode (runs on view switch only, not during drag). Fix: add auto-classification to the collab mutation code path, likely in updateContextPosition or as a post-drag handler similar to completeFlowStageMove.

title: Excessive tooltip layering on relationship edge hover
description: Hovering a context relationship edge shows three overlapping affordances for the same fact: (1) the inline pattern label on the edge ("Partnership"), (2) a native browser <title> tooltip ("partnership") from <title>{pattern}</title> at src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx:328, (3) a custom portal-rendered educational tooltip (dark card with title, description, characteristics) at src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx:330-371. The native <title> also violates the project rule in CLAUDE.md ("NEVER use native title attribute"). Proposed fix: remove the <title> element entirely; keep the educational portal tooltip but only on first-time hover or when showHelpTooltips is on, and consider a short delay so it does not stack visually with the inline label. Alternatively, suppress the educational tooltip on hover when the edge already has a visible inline label, and reserve it for the indicator-box patterns (ACL/OHS) where the meaning is less obvious. Goal: one clear affordance per hover, not three.

title: Connection UX redesign: hover stubs, whole-shape drop targets (GH #22)
description:
Redesign relationship creation UX for the DDD Europe 2026 workshop audience. The current implementation renders two fixed source-handle dots on each context's left and right edges; users must precisely target them to start a connection, and the resulting edge's display geometry disagrees with where they dragged from. This bead replaces that with discoverable hover affordances and a forgiving drop target.

Three implementation changes:

1) Hover stubs (draw.io style). On hover, show directional arrow nubs on all 4 sides of a context (top, right, bottom, left). Nubs are translucent (~35% opacity) by default and pop to full opacity plus accent color on individual hover. A small tooltip appears on stub hover: "Drag to another context to map the relationship". Drag from any stub starts a connection. Replaces the existing fixed left/right source-handle dots.

2) Whole-shape drop target. While a connection-drag is in flight, dropping anywhere on the body of a destination context completes the connection. Implemented as a single transparent full-size React Flow target Handle behind the visible card so React Flow's existing hit-testing accepts the drop. The displayed endpoint floats to the closest side via the joint-anchor algorithm from #21. Body-drag of a card continues to move it: connection-drag and node-drag stay distinct because connection-drag is initiated by a stub mousedown, node-drag by a body mousedown.

3) Bezier curves preserved. The hand-drawn/organic feel is intentional for strategic-design work; orthogonal routing was considered and rejected. No change to path type.

Coupled deliverables that ship in the same PR:

- Arrow marker size: bump relationship arrow markers from markerWidth/markerHeight 5 to 8 (markers defined in src/components/CanvasArea.tsx around lines 1063-1119, all five marker variants). Reads better at workshop zoom levels.
- Rich card-hover tooltip guidance line: update the line in src/lib/contextTooltip.ts that currently reads "Drag handles to connect to other contexts" to "Drag any arrow to another context to map the relationship." This help text describes the changing affordance and will go stale if we ship the new affordance without updating it.

Key files to touch (orientation, not prescription): src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx (replace fixed source handles with hover stubs, add per-stub tooltip), src/components/CanvasArea.tsx (marker defs, onConnect flow), src/lib/contextTooltip.ts (guidance line wording), src/lib/edgeGeometry.ts (already correct via #21; no change expected). Existing User -> User Need -> Context flow uses the top target handle and must keep working.

Background and decisions: docs/connection-ux-research.md and docs/mockups/connection-ux-mockup.html. These currently live on the mockup/connection-ux branch (htmlpreview link on GH #22 comment); both will land in the implementation PR or its predecessor merge.

Direction convention is kept as-is for #22 (drag-from = downstream, drag-to = upstream; arrow points to upstream, as documented in src/model/types.ts:80-86). Reopen during two-sided pattern work (contextflow-ki1 / GH #13). All copy on the new creation surfaces is intentionally direction-neutral so symmetric patterns (Partnership, Shared Kernel, Separate Ways, which render with no arrow head) read correctly.

Acceptance criteria (observable behavior):
- Hovering a context reveals 4 translucent arrow nubs on top/right/bottom/left.
- Hovering one nub pops it to full opacity and shows tooltip "Drag to another context to map the relationship".
- Dragging from any nub starts a connection-drag with a ghost line following the cursor.
- Dropping on any point of a destination context's body (not just a handle) creates the relationship.
- Body-drag of a context (not on a nub) moves the context; no relationship is created.
- The displayed edge endpoint floats to the closest side per #21 (no regression).
- Relationship arrow markers render visibly larger than before (markerWidth/markerHeight 8 vs prior 5).
- The rich card-hover tooltip (gated by showHelpTooltips) shows updated guidance: "Drag any arrow to another context to map the relationship." No reference to "handles" remains.
- Existing User -> User Need -> Context connection flow still works.
- Symmetric patterns (Partnership, Shared Kernel, Separate Ways) still render with no arrow head.
- The original two fixed left/right source-handle dots are no longer rendered.

Separate decision flagged for the user (not in current scope):
- First-run coach mark teaching the gesture. This is an onboarding overlay, not a tooltip. Discussed during mockup review. Ask before pulling in.

See GH issue #22 for product framing and workshop motivation. Depends on contextflow-j7k (closed: joint anchor side selection for floating edges).
notes:
Mockup phase complete (2026-05-21). Branch: mockup/connection-ux. Htmlpreview link on GH #22 comment.

Memory entries to consult during implementation:
- design_bezier_edges.md (path type)
- design_arrow_marker_size.md (5 -> 8)
- design_drag_direction_convention.md (P1 kept for #22; revisit in ki1)
- feedback_visual_mockups_first.md (process)
- feedback_tooltips_ship_with_feature.md (why the rich tooltip update is in scope, not deferred)

Status: ready to start implementation (TDD). Outer-loop scenarios should follow the acceptance criteria list in the description.

title: Fix bezier line and arrow marker overlap at edge endpoint (GH #24)
description: The bezier path runs to the destination anchor on the box edge, and the arrow marker is drawn on top, causing line+marker visual overlap. Path should stop at the back of the arrow triangle so only the point touches the box. Two implementation options: (1) geometrically shorten the path by arrow length in RelationshipEdge.tsx or getEdgeParams, or (2) configure SVG marker refX to position the marker base at the path endpoint. Independent of #21 but sequencing-wise queue after #21. See GH #24.

title: Set up Cloudflare billing usage notifications
description: After exceeding the free tier Durable Objects limits (2026-02-26), upgraded to paid plan. Need to configure billing usage alerts in Cloudflare dashboard to avoid unexpected charges. Set up notifications for Workers, Durable Objects, and Pages usage thresholds.

title: ARCHITECTURE.md: add Distillation View throughout
description:
ARCHITECTURE.md was written when only two views existed (Flow and Strategic). Distillation View is missing everywhere:
- Overview says 'two synchronized projections' - should be three
- ViewMode type in EditorState section shows only 'flow' | 'strategic' - missing 'distillation'
- TopBar description says 'Toggle Flow View / Strategic View' - missing Distillation
- Canvas axes section missing Distillation View description. Actual axes per DistillationRegions.tsx: X-axis = Business Differentiation (low to high), Y-axis = Model Complexity (low to high), with quadrant regions (Generic, Supporting, Core)
- Canvas drag behavior section missing Distillation (allows independent 2D positioning via positions.distillation.x and positions.distillation.y)
- canvasView in EditorState missing distillation viewport

title: Audit and remove dead action functions superseded by collab mutations
description: The store was refactored to use collab mutations (getCollabMutations()) but the old action functions in src/model/actions/ still exist with stale trackEvent calls. context_added, user_added, user_need_added events were silently dropped because the store methods were the real code path. Audit all action files (contextActions.ts, userActions.ts, etc.) to identify which functions are dead code vs still called, and remove the dead ones. Single source of truth should be the store methods.
close_reason: Deleted 4 dead action files (contextActions, userActions, teamActions, temporalActions) plus their tests, removed unused userActions import from store.ts. 3,352 lines removed. projectActions, projectHelpers, and keyframeHelpers kept (still actively used).
